channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A Practical Guide to Getting Fit in Midlife
Preventive Health Tests to Know After 50
Savoring the Journey with Padma Lakshmi
item.description: The Emmy-nominated TV host dishes on the power of connection, food, fitness, and the art of finding joy.

item.title: Higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden may predict postoperative cardiovascular complications and mortality in obstructive sleep apnea
item.description: 1. In this cohort of patients with Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), a higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden (SASHB) was associated with a significa...

item.title: Targeting a dangerous gut infection: Studies reveal how C. diff behaves inside the body
item.description: Affecting roughly half a million Americans each year, bacterial infections caused by Clostridioides difficile—commonly known as C. diff—are a serious ...

item.title: How board certification fuels the physician shortage crisis
item.description: For years, the conversation about board certification has centered on fairness, cost, and evidence. But there is another consequence that gets far les...

item.title: Oral remibrutinib hits Phase 3 primary endpoint for inducible hives
item.description: 1. Oral remibrutinib successfully met its primary endpoint in the Phase III RemIND trial, delivering significant symptom relief for chronic inducible ...

item.title: Study confirms food fortification is highly cost-effective in fighting hidden hunger across 63 countries
item.description: A comprehensive new systematic review published in The Journal of Nutrition provides the latest evidence that large-scale food fortification is a high...

item.title: Healthy Diet May Cut Cancer Survivors’ Premature Aging Risk
item.description: A recent study found reduced premature aging among childhood cancer survivors that adhered to established dietary guidelines. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Rest is a holy practice: Reclaiming the soul of medicine [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Developmental behavioral pediatrician Roxanne Almas discusses her art...

item.title: Study maps US diabetes–cancer hotspots
item.description: A new University of Kentucky study has mapped areas across the U.S. where high rates of diabetes and deaths from diabetes-related cancers overlap. The...

item.title: First global 'early warning system' for dengue launches
item.description: A new online dashboard will give researchers, governments, and the public a real-time picture of the global dengue situation for the first time. The G...

item.title: Virtual therapy strengthens social skills in autism, Simville study finds
item.description: An increasing number of people worldwide are affected by autism spectrum disorder (ASD); according to studies, one in 44 children is diagnosed with it...

item.title: Lobectomy: Viable Alternative in Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma
item.description: Thyroid lobectomy may provide oncologic outcomes similar to those of total thyroidectomy in patients with sporadic medullary thyroid carcinoma, per a ...

item.title: Chronic Rhinosinusitis Tied to Increased Cancer Risk
item.description: Results from Asia suggest chronic rhinosinusitis is associated with an increased risk for cancer, but whether these results apply to the US population...

item.title: Can Physical Activity Improve Skin Cancer Outcomes?
item.description: Physical activity is associated with a reduced risk for all-cause mortality and improved survival in patients with skin cancer, a meta-analysis shows....

item.title: Bariatric Surgery Rates Fall as GLP-1s Gain Popularity
item.description: The use of GLP 1s increased dramatically from 2018 to 2025 among patients eligible for metabolic and bariatric surgery in the US, whereas surgery rate...

item.title: Merck and Mayo Clinic launch AI-driven precision medicine lab

item.title: Lifestyle factors including higher body mass index and smoking are associated with changes in left-atrial size and function
item.description: 1. In this prospective cohort study, lifestyle behaviours, especially higher body mass index (BMI) and smoking, are associated with measurable changes...

item.title: Why I left the surgical-trauma ICU: a nurse’s story of burnout
item.description: After working close to 20 years in this medical-surgical ICU, I thought I was an expert. Infallible. I could do it all. I left my ICU comfort zone, my...

item.title: Rebuilding patient trust through the evolutionary mismatch framework
item.description: How one reframing changed the way my patients think about me, and their health. Every day, a patient sits across from me, arms crossed, waiting for me...

item.title: American health care policy reform: Why we need a bipartisan commission
item.description: America is ready for an honest conversation about health care policy before more people suffer and we go broke waiting for it to happen. Fully one-thi...

item.title: Smartphone-based digital ruler achieved high diagnostic performance for strabismus screening
